name: xarray (N-D Labeled Arrays)

auth.notes: Local library; no auth; cloud storage accessed via fsspec

performance.notes: Lazy evaluation with Dask backend; handles petabyte-scale datasets via chunking

agent_readiness.notes: N-dimensional labeled arrays for scientific computing. Extends NumPy/Pandas to multi-dimensional data with named dimensions and coordinates. NetCDF, Zarr, HDF5 file format support. Dask integration for out-of-core computation. Essential for climate science, meteorology, oceanography, geospatial raster analysis. DataArray (single variable) and Dataset (multiple variables). GroupBy, rolling, resampling, interpolation operations. Widely used in geosciences and environmental modeling.

gotchas: Learning curve for users unfamiliar with labeled N-D arrays
Memory usage can be high without Dask chunking
Some operations require understanding broadcasting semantics
Less familiar API than pandas for 2D data
best_when: Climate, weather, oceanographic, and geospatial raster data
Out-of-core computation on large arrays (Dask backend)
summary: xarray provides N-dimensional labeled arrays for scientific computing — pandas for N-D data. Extends NumPy with named dimensions and coordinates. NetCDF/Zarr/HDF5 support. Dask integration for petabyte-scale out-of-core computation. The standard for climate science, meteorology, and geospatial raster analysis. Essential for multidimensional scientific datasets with coordinates and metadata.
